**Sunday, August 23, 2015**

This week, Senator Paul took a break from campaigning to visit Cap-Haitien, Haiti. During the trip, sponsored by the University of Utah's Moran Eye Center, he and other doctors performed sight-restoring eye surgeries for local citizens. Dr. Paul continued his long-held tradition of helping those in need with his ophthalmological experience. You can [support the Moran Eye Center](http://paracom.paramountcommunication.com/ct/29934785:28687595609:m:1:879035888:58EA20C3DF2EE30C2D3F14903659B0E7:r) on their website.

Looking ahead, Senator Paul will be making stops in Alaska, Washington, Idaho, Wyoming, and Utah this week. Be sure to check our [Events Calendar](http://paracom.paramountcommunication.com/ct/29934786:28687595609:m:1:879035888:58EA20C3DF2EE30C2D3F14903659B0E7:r) to find out when Rand is coming to your area.

Chip Englander
Campaign Manager
Rand Paul for President

## News of the Week

| | |
### How To Fix A Broken Eye With GOP Presidential Candidate Rand Paul
Kentucky Senator and presidential hopeful Rand Paul has changed the August game. While the rest of the field flipped pork chops in Iowa this week, Rand Paul could be found 2,000 miles away, flipping cataracts out of eyeballs. Paul has become the first presidential candidate in history to conduct surgery during a bid for the White House.

[ ](http://paracom.paramountcommunication.com/ct/29934787:28687595609:m:1:879035888:58EA20C3DF2EE30C2D3F14903659B0E7:r)
---
|
### Rand Paul: A Humanitarian In Haiti, A Pit Bull In 2016
Even while fighting blindness in the poorest nation in the Western Hemisphere this week, Republican presidential contender Rand Paul intensified political attacks against rivals in both parties, vowing to continue pressing billionaire businessman Donald Trump in particular as the Kentucky senator embraces the role as the GOP's leading pit bull.

[ ](http://paracom.paramountcommunication.com/ct/29934788:28687595609:m:1:879035888:58EA20C3DF2EE30C2D3F14903659B0E7:r)
---
|
### Op-Ed: The Federal Reserve is Not Your Friend
Perversely, members of Congress have to fight an uphill battle just to audit the Federal Reserve. We do not want to politicize monetary policy (as our detractors allege), but rather simply shine a very bright light on this unaccountable and unchecked (and thus entirely un-American) power. By doing this, we may finally be able to rein it in.

name: Patrick Murphy
subject: Petition: Reverse Citizens United
disclaimer_text: paid for by groups with names like "Americans for Freedom" and "Prosperity of a Greater America GPS
body:
| Friend,

We've all seen the political ads that come up in an election year. It's hard to miss them. They're full of dark images and sinister-sounding music. And they're paid for by groups with names like "Americans for Freedom" and "Prosperity of a Greater America GPS."

Of course, these groups aren't backed by everyday Americans -- they're backed by billionaires and corporations. These groups funnel huge sums of money, many times without disclosure, and dump it into elections to influence voters.

How do they get away with it? Two words: _**Citizens United.**_

The Supreme Court opened the spending door with that decision back in 2010 -- **and special interest groups have burst right through, spending more and more as time goes on.**

We need to slam the door shut again -- and soon. [**Join me in telling Congress: I am an American, I am against _Citizens United_. And I want you to take immediate action to reverse the disastrous effects it has had on our democracy.**](http://fl.murphyforflorida.com/page/m/10ae2026/738133e4/3adcdb18/7f97f09d/439530064/VEsH/ "http://fl.murphyforflorida.com/e150824b")

The best example of the damage _Citizens United_ set in motion is the Koch brothers' network of outside groups.

In the 2014 elections, the Koch network spent $100 million and ran more than 44,000 ads. They're planning on going even bigger in the 2016 elections -- **they 've pledged to spend almost a BILLION dollars.**

When groups can spend that much, their issues get heard most. Which is a problem, because **the issues these groups care about are NOT the same issues Florida families care about.**

The Koch brothers, and their big-money donors, get one vote each -- just like everyone else. Their voices shouldn't be heard a billion times louder than any other American. Their issues shouldn't dominate elections across the country.

This isn't the United States of Koch & Friends. This is the United States of America. And this isn't how our democracy should work.

[**It will take a lot of work to bring some transparency, accountability, and, dare I say, sanity back to our campaign finance system -- but we can do it. We just need to work together.

Click here today to make your voice heard.**](http://fl.murphyforflorida.com/page/m/10ae2026/738133e4/3adcdb18/7f97f09d/439530064/VEsE/ "http://fl.murphyforflorida.com/e150824b")

I know what it's like to face outside spending -- I've seen a lot of it. I'm sure I'll face more than my fair share this time around, too.

But I'm not going to back down until we put an end to the damage of _Citizens United_.

Thanks for standing with me today.

Patrick | | | | | Paid for by Friends of Patrick Murphy | 4521 PGA Blvd #412, Palm Beach Gardens, FL 33418 | [http://murphyforflorida.com](http://fl.murphyforflorida.com/page/m/10ae2026/738133e4/3adcdb18/7f97f09e/439530064/VEsF/ "http://murphyforflorida.com/")

Courageous Conservatives - Reigniting the Promise of America
**Hello Friend,**
Wow -- what an inspiration Friday night was!
In Des Moines, IA, Ted hosted a [Rally for Religious Liberty](http://em.tedcruz.org/wf/click?upn=oxRiKmNZf-2B-2FjHRySYBDLy09v5edMMZm9mVbjZI6Snjy8EawkTk-2FHZZlL14ZURzbUn5LJdbZYzkbSdVtznixRhSCEt7WcaJhVyyekWghqlLxFe5hDRYT9OerRXUZAirWOieTITfYO-2BsNMppGmr6LYeQ-3D-3D_enNSH-2FxHAAqXgxiHRFihpX91d75ERAkpfg4XQfmYhCFTWivT-2BkGtpyy56lu7EQCKpA8oCgALny1OkafEWRPX5yBBgye2YmWMRjr6IGjVEgy6pSh21NWmaeDEQqSvtAqqIxzwv-2BFw0P9v9RFpGGsdUJ-2F8ntxJggWJWUWaNFdWS4lrymEhCbi6dng-2B-2BJYo-2FjUwxF7UEzey0d4WjtXjI2D8idt7L-2FHIUf5DCuh-2BToYFlNtR31DtzNB0pInirkK-2BpbF2h8MhAyOsIM3k-2BI2Jg24Zz9w1dUnkTy1IjuH5BvMYxOUM82OBHHnyr76KDsv5yq8oYMhC7IOe8PUiBvkFZItevf05uXxOn80MEtRxcNEWBqYs3h1XjyUeSo5bMUs8SYWJ40jWvzUMy9vQrMGS0wAiddvg8S9Yw8a4Id0Vc8qRrDo-3D) calling attention to the religious persecution of Christian business owners and employees who have been sanctioned by their government because of their religious beliefs. Joining Ted at the rally were: Dick and Betty Odgaard, Barronelle Stutzman, Aaron and Melissa Klein, Blaine Adamson, Senior Master Sergeant (Retired) Phillip Monk, Former Atlanta Fire Chief Kelvin Cochran, and Naghmeh Abedini.
You can watch [video of the rally here](http://em.tedcruz.org/wf/click?upn=TEeb5-2FMuQyoelyZpsAGcFDNfYrr00HXoM-2BFrq9OgYfg2Izxs5S5ubrg-2FABraTkDiHmfSwHzu-2Bbcc1reOwc9rGO8kqminC1cr5EsBxv6feoMR5YXrDRhwrVIIi7qAvfvDDwj0VYlw6P9njVMgx1Gkjw-3D-3D_enNSH-2FxHAAqXgxiHRFihpX91d75ERAkpfg4XQfmYhCFTWivT-2BkGtpyy56lu7EQCKnh3-2FomJ6WPY9P7bdC-2FNBg2ls0v67Ygxwp3B65zitcaIv0ExZ0WXEnm6CoYVHcw-2FUoTfCommtak3SBFxAupBa9ssomvM3uJJQV1-2FuXVNTQV-2F-2Bi6Os3ZF8HyCmFI4-2FV72y4zftV0WT-2FPGZDhnvF73Svm1qO-2BVHAx3g02g55moj-2Fz29-2FkJxI5tMMG7vz6xenLjpmpJhj8RERn0j63cBAaojP8uXe4HyusVVyo-2Fr4eRj8mYkwld2Dy8YFnv63cG6xJ6OVs8fG6yKmvIlo7ptL19UvJ5qKuYByMJX9UnF5Cq7wbZDFJpubJB6sN7f2xW7vJmu5j7Dv0AlFHSA81QXs467Ir39GQCtdSXr1JpzTMjr-2FWk-3D):
[](http://em.tedcruz.org/wf/click?upn=TEeb5-2FMuQyoelyZpsAGcFDNfYrr00HXoM-2BFrq9OgYfg2Izxs5S5ubrg-2FABraTkDiHmfSwHzu-2Bbcc1reOwc9rGO8kqminC1cr5EsBxv6feoMR5YXrDRhwrVIIi7qAvfvDDwj0VYlw6P9njVMgx1Gkjw-3D-3D_enNSH-2FxHAAqXgxiHRFihpX91d75ERAkpfg4XQfmYhCFTWivT-2BkGtpyy56lu7EQCKkF2I-2BE9zmtZre2Rr0eo3uctyKapBQCTC2IUBDCysXPdNzLEDDy-2FLGE51MhTqtrg8UpqYmNBSHiDlsuNSZ5wMs0y8O1WK7V-2BIWd4fxZzt-2B7qN8qllkx5oHDCdIiDU-2FCExj-2B-2BLhqWSU0sgVUzVSEKu-2BhUIjSNeSdVM-2B-2Bi5nI-2BXhH-2BKdWWVzCno0Z3V-2Fjt7ifgCxU3D1hA4Xm4r518U340AL7LbrHz-2BcIKp-2BKovZuLTLGnDrHUvoOrAVHfbVm7IQ6I2197Tk0hA12-2FoJdWPw47xdi9iZ-2BSFYVjY2aIOmMPXMEQiwe1MaCvArEoOFYZOilMd4OXStFgHheyWHcqmwLEKpJR-2FW5EhuGzsSmOCEc8iraY-3D)
Also hosting several of the victims of religious persecution for a discussion was Iowa-based syndicated radio host and outspoken conservative activist Steve Deace. Steve has been a fixture in Iowa broadcasting for more than a decade and is an influential opinion maker across the Hawkeye State, is a tireless advocate for liberty, fiscal responsibility, and traditional American values, and is considered by many one of the rising stars in conservative media.
Earlier this week, Steve [announced on his radio show](http://em.tedcruz.org/wf/click?upn=oxRiKmNZf-2B-2FjHRySYBDLy09v5edMMZm9mVbjZI6SnjwU4r9WiGc4P-2BrZoRjGruihIYJfx60IGE6J3ys-2BWxSKa9IcHwX0qmMrxSU-2F-2FD1ITI4-3D_enNSH-2FxHAAqXgxiHRFihpX91d75ERAkpfg4XQfmYhCFTWivT-2BkGtpyy56lu7EQCKIcoNYJXFhWXhLfzFu1D3up0T5MRpAOwV2Ov8ZQU0VZrO6pX9N14N1DRQctqosUsyDsX05BRMcT8ScjHaG8qBVpQsuOXBgY5NLHhOQ0gIii2p8CaWjdYjaWNivD8BLLAskZAmmaj7FnsT0kwZ8tRAUO8HWygcXOzkmsAOB-2F5M07fDUjlA5l9Q-2F2vIrc8sY15tcQq1Oqa-2FXK6Y1A2EPmfhhlflRSJUYLIGv-2FI68FslAhhUmFwhHFkDwOc27J14jqHNmejP00zK23t7-2BrHWkA6X6gAn6dyD1tMFN-2FaBf3TP9JnwMEzLf7tkUea3NUKXmQ1QNSFq6Mwcexi034iuF-2FEIDxWTXhZPu1B4-2BUzrZz7P6wc-3D) that he will be supporting Ted for President. He also [wrote about the endorsement in the _Washington Times_](http://em.tedcruz.org/wf/click?upn=oxRiKmNZf-2B-2FjHRySYBDLy09v5edMMZm9mVbjZI6SnjwMn9fROGgY9Ht1Sfw5k2m6xH2ID457yOd9JSycd5V83qWQmpv5h0H-2BqrtdcYyzM2U-2FCjv9TngEU1iSnDv7KIBa_enNSH-2FxHAAqXgxiHRFihpX91d75ERAkpfg4XQfmYhCFTWivT-2BkGtpyy56lu7EQCKWpUUmTxqC70kV4irLICUjGx2jrE0a1Cc2RH4NbvpwweeKaPSo-2FYgKOdLTqBdBWYo6Hf1fakHKx2-2BosyTk-2FoVtx8AOdK7VM7tI3LyyQ9JA7J7m-2FLoc4YZcdOIBDJLJpxyDT8is7LbfxCVrI-2FYF-2B1a-2FJFTksq6pRhGHb8uCiiYyIgDsWToUriFSdkdz4RT-2F6m9rjEBPeUZpVjMIP-2BvfXqSE5S0Rw6N9UvSXuoRmrQSBBjSm1-2FOwQ5pdp6m63hnFrLbrEElzWiCk31hj8L4QR7ZQ4SINPlTJgrr9BlMqFCe5qRwIs-2BilPfBBDLv7JBmQWOmJUZpOfJOku9RBg8NEq-2Ffg3dvz9XDDAoNugmbpxd0qfk-3D) saying:
_[T]here are three things that set Mr. Cruz apart from the rest which deem him worthy of an early endorsement._
_First, Mr. Cruz is the one candidate in this primary who has never looked the other way when the GOP establishment sold out conservatives or betrayed the trust of the American people..._
_Second for Mr. Cruz is electability. Starting with the primary, conservatives can only coalesce to defeat the GOP establishment if there is a viable, full-spectrum conservative in the race. And not only can no one question Mr. Cruz 's conservative credentials, but he has raised the most hard money of any GOP presidential candidate (and an astonishing $50 million overall). He's already drawing massive crowds and building organization in southern Super Tuesday states that surpass what most of his campaign rivals have on the ground in the first in the nation caucus state of Iowa. The sum of those parts is proof Mr. Cruz is the only conservative candidate ready to run a national campaign against the establishment - something conservatives haven't been able to do since Ronald Reagan..._
_Third and finally, it 's not just enough to have courage of conviction and the resources to win. You must be able to lead the country once elected, and this debate isn't as simple as choosing between a governor or senator to helm the office of president..._
_And that is why I am proud to endorse him for president of the United States, and will be doing everything in my power to encourage courageous conservatives across the country to do the same._
Ted is leading this fight to defend our religious liberty because he recognizes this nation was founded by brave men and women fleeing religious oppression who came to a new world where they could seek and worship the Lord God almighty with all of their heart, mind, and soul -- free of oppression from government.
Sadly, under the Obama Administration, we are seeing the greatest assault on faith in our nation's history. The persecution of religious liberty must end. clean_body: Courageous Conservatives - Reigniting the Promise of America Hello Friend, Wow -- what an inspiration Friday night was! In Des Moines, IA, Ted hosted a calling attention to the religious persecution of Christian business owners and employees who have been sanctioned by their government because of their religious beliefs. Joining Ted at the rally were: Dick and Betty Odgaard, Barronelle Stutzman, Aaron and Melissa Klein, Blaine Adamson, Senior Master Sergeant (Retired) Phillip Monk, Former Atlanta Fire Chief Kelvin Cochran, and Naghmeh Abedini. You can watch : Also hosting several of the victims of religious persecution for a discussion was Iowa-based syndicated radio host and outspoken conservative activist Steve Deace. Steve has been a fixture in Iowa broadcasting for more than a decade and is an influential opinion maker across the Hawkeye State, is a tireless advocate for liberty, fiscal responsibility, and traditional American values, and is considered by many one of the rising stars in conservative media. Earlier this week, Steve that he will be supporting Ted for President. He also saying: [T]here are three things that set Mr. Cruz apart from the rest which deem him worthy of an early endorsement. First, Mr. Cruz is the one candidate in this primary who has never looked the other way when the GOP establishment sold out conservatives or betrayed the trust of the American people... Second for Mr. Cruz is electability. Starting with the primary, conservatives can only coalesce to defeat the GOP establishment if there is a viable, full-spectrum conservative in the race. And not only can no one question Mr. Cruz 's conservative credentials, but he has raised the most hard money of any GOP presidential candidate (and an astonishing $50 million overall). He's already drawing massive crowds and building organization in southern Super Tuesday states that surpass what most of his campaign rivals have on the ground in the first in the nation caucus state of Iowa. The sum of those parts is proof Mr. Cruz is the only conservative candidate ready to run a national campaign against the establishment - something conservatives haven't been able to do since Ronald Reagan... Third and finally, it 's not just enough to have courage of conviction and the resources to win. You must be able to lead the country once elected, and this debate isn't as simple as choosing between a governor or senator to helm the office of president... And that is why I am proud to endorse him for president of the United States, and will be doing everything in my power to encourage courageous conservatives across the country to do the same. Ted is leading this fight to defend our religious liberty because he recognizes this nation was founded by brave men and women fleeing religious oppression who came to a new world where they could seek and worship the Lord God almighty with all of their heart, mind, and soul -- free of oppression from government. Sadly, under the Obama Administration, we are seeing the greatest assault on faith in our nation's history. The persecution of religious liberty must end. ? We're looking forward to another great week here at the Cruz for President HQ, and we hope you are too! name: Rand Paul
subject: I applaud the Republican Party of Kentucky
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Rand Paul for President
body:
Fellow Conservative,

Yesterday, the Republican Party of Kentucky voted in favor of holding a caucus for the upcoming 2016 presidential election.

My campaign released a statement on this important decision, and I wanted to share it with you:

* * *
### Rand Paul Applauds the Republican Party of Kentucky

**WASHINGTON, D.C. -** The Republican Party of Kentucky today voted in favor of holding a caucus for the upcoming 2016 presidential election. Following this decision, Sen. Rand Paul issued a statement supporting the efforts of the Kentucky state party:

"I applaud the Republican Party of Kentucky on their decision to hold a caucus in the upcoming Republican presidential cycle. The people of Kentucky deserve a voice as the GOP chooses their next nominee, and holding a caucus will ensure that Kentucky is relevant and participates early in the process. I am also grateful for the Republican Party's trust in me, allowing me to run for re-election to the U.S. Senate and seek the nomination for the Presidency of the United States," Sen. Rand Paul said.
* * *

In Liberty,

Rand Paul

P.S. **[Please chip in a contribution of $20.16 to help me defeat the Washington Machine and take our country back](https://secure.randpaul.com/?movementbegins&sr=rhf082315nd)**!
